title: Medium Priority Integrations
impact: Medium - Extends TaskScheduler integration to ArchiveManager, SemanticSearch, and TransactionManager

archiveProgress.current: No progress reporting during archive operations
archiveProgress.target: Progress callback for entity evaluation and archival

semanticSearchCancellation.current: indexAll has onProgress but no cancellation
semanticSearchCancellation.target: Full AbortSignal support for indexAll and search operations

tasks.title: Integrate TaskScheduler with ArchiveManager
tasks.description: Add progress callback and cancellation support to ArchiveManager.archiveEntities() method. This enables tracking progress during large archive operations.

implementation.purpose: Enable progress tracking and cancellation during entity archival, improving UX for large archive operations.
keyDecisions: Report progress in two phases: evaluation (0-50%) and archival (50-100%)
Support cancellation between phases and during archival
Return enhanced result with cancellation info

currentCode.signature: async archiveEntities(criteria: ArchiveCriteria, options: ArchiveOptions | boolean = {}): Promise<ArchiveResult>

stepByStep.details: Add: import { type AsyncOperationOptions, checkAborted } from '../types/index.js';

stepByStep.action: Add progress reporting during entity evaluation
stepByStep.details:
In the evaluation loop (lines 117-149), report progress: opts.onProgress?.(entityIndex, total, 'Evaluating entities');
Scale to 0-50% range: const progress = Math.floor((entityIndex / total) * 50);
Every 100 entities: if (entityIndex % 100 === 0) checkAborted(opts.signal, 'archiveEntities');

After saveToArchive completes: opts.onProgress?.(75, 100, 'Writing archive file');
After graph save: opts.onProgress?.(100, 100, 'Archive complete');

stepByStep.action: Add cancellation check before archive write
stepByStep.details: Before calling saveToArchive: checkAborted(opts.signal, 'archiveEntities');
Wrap in try-catch. If cancelled, return: { archived: 0, entityNames: [], cancelled: true }
Add to interface: cancelled?: boolean;

stepByStep.action: Add tests for progress and cancellation
stepByStep.details: Test progress callback is called during both phases, test cancellation stops operation

acceptanceCriteria: Progress callback receives updates during evaluation (0-50%) and archival (50-100%)
AbortSignal cancellation stops the operation at safe points
Legacy calls with boolean parameter still work

tasks.title: Add AbortSignal Support to SemanticSearch
tasks.description: Enhance SemanticSearch.indexAll() to support AbortSignal cancellation. The method already has onProgress callback via SemanticIndexOptions, but needs cancellation support.

implementation.purpose: Enable cancellation of semantic indexing operations, which can be slow for large graphs due to embedding API calls.
keyDecisions: Check cancellation between batches in indexAll
Support cancellation in search method for long-running queries
Return indexed count in case of cancellation

currentCode.signature: async indexAll(graph: ReadonlyKnowledgeGraph, options: SemanticIndexOptions = {}): Promise<{ indexed: number; skipped: number; errors: number }>

stepByStep.details: Add signal field: signal?: AbortSignal;
Add: import { checkAborted } from '../types/index.js'; (after we add it to types/async.ts)

stepByStep.action: Add cancellation check in indexAll batch loop
stepByStep.details: At the start of the for loop processing batches (line 126): checkAborted(options.signal, 'indexAll');

stepByStep.action: Add 'cancelled' field to indexAll return type
stepByStep.details: Change return type to: Promise<{ indexed: number; skipped: number; errors: number; cancelled?: boolean }>
Wrap batch processing in try-catch. If cancelled, return current stats with cancelled: true

stepByStep.action: Add cancellation support to search method
stepByStep.details:
Add optional signal parameter: async search(graph, query, limit?, minSimilarity?, signal?: AbortSignal)
Check cancellation after embedding generation: checkAborted(signal, 'semanticSearch');
Test that aborting signal mid-indexing returns partial results with cancelled: true
Test that aborting during search throws cancellation error

acceptanceCriteria: indexAll returns cancelled: true when aborted

tasks.title: Integrate TaskScheduler with TransactionManager
tasks.description: Add progress callback support to TransactionManager.commit() method. This enables tracking progress during transaction execution, which is useful for transactions with many operations.

implementation.purpose: Enable progress tracking during transaction commits, showing operation-by-operation progress for large transactions.
keyDecisions: Add CommitOptions interface with progress callback
Report progress after each operation in commit()
Do NOT support cancellation mid-commit (atomic semantics)
implementation.atomicityNote: Transactions must be atomic - either all operations succeed or all fail. Therefore, we do NOT support cancellation during commit. The user should call rollback() if they want to cancel before commit.

stepByStep.details: Add: import { type ProgressCallback } from '../types/index.js';
async commit(options?: CommitOptions): Promise<TransactionResult>

stepByStep.action: Add progress reporting in operation loop

stepByStep.details: Before starting: options?.onProgress?.(0, this.operations.length, 'Starting transaction');
After save: options?.onProgress?.(operationsExecuted, this.operations.length, 'Transaction committed');
Test that progress callback receives calls for each operation in transaction

acceptanceCriteria: CommitOptions interface exists with onProgress callback
Progress callback receives updates after each operation
Progress includes operation type in message
Note: No cancellation support (by design for atomic semantics)

successCriteria: All three tasks completed with passing tests
notes: Sprint 2 depends on Sprint 1 Task 9B.1.1 for async types
TransactionManager intentionally does NOT support cancellation during commit
SemanticSearch already has onProgress, just needs signal added
ArchiveManager progress should reflect both evaluation and archival phases
Test with mock embedding service for SemanticSearch tests to avoid API calls
